note: Honest coverage frontier. status: present = a runnable step drives real code and passes; pending = specified behavior whose implementation (core/suppression.Chain and its registries, Phase 2) does not exist yet; retrospective_gap = shipped without a driving test (must be zero for governed behavior). All scenarios are pending until core/suppression is built.

scenarios.name: A phase exception fails open to escalation
An unconfirmed suppression match fails open to escalation
A critical-severity reboot is never suppressed
An unknown-severity alert is never suppressed
A future-dated triage entry is rejected and the alert escalates
A negative-age triage entry is rejected and the alert escalates
A prior entry within the window is accepted for dedup evaluation
A currently-valid suppression-policy record activates the fold
An active fold posts the child alert as a notice without a session
An expired suppression-policy record fails open
A stale-verified suppression-policy record fails open
An on-schedule reboot with a live registered schedule is suppressed
An observing schedule does not suppress
A reboot outside the schedule window escalates
A critical reboot with a matching schedule is not suppressed
A host-agnostic transient rule matches by alert rule
A transient rule does not match a different alert rule
A dirty boot after a suppressed reboot reopens and pages
A clean systemd-reboot stays suppressed
A schedule promotes to live after two in-window boots
A single in-window boot does not promote the schedule
A removed schedule drifts to disabled
